routineText:
09:00 — Wake up & go to Work
09:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
13:00
13:00 — Black coffee + 1 tsp of sugar

-----------------------
| 14:00 — 🌅 Breakfast
| — whole wheat bread 35g
| — 6 whites & 3 yolks of beldi eggs fried with 5g of olive oil
| — 5g of raw olive oil
| — mint tea + 1 tsp of sugar
| — cumin & salt
| — oat 55g + warm water (↑ from 35g)
| — 1 scoop of protein powder with water
| — 1 apple (small, ~150g)
| — 2 garlic cloves
-----------------------

14:00 — return to Work
14:00
      — (4h) Work & Water 1L
18:00
18:00 — Out from work

-----------------------
| 18:00 — ☀️ Lunch
| — chicken breast 200g raw cooked with 1 tsp of mustard and 5g of olive oil
| — brown rice 25g raw
| — 300g legume sauté with 5g of olive oil (40% carrot, 40% courgette, 20% onion)
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

18:00 — Black Coffee
18:00
      — (4h) Rest or Billard & Water 1L
21:00
21:00 — Back Home

-----------------------
| 21:00 — 🌙 Dinner
| — 1.5 scoops of protein powder (↑ from 1)
| — 1 perly nature
| — 10g Almonds & Walnuts & Cashews & Pistachios (2.5g each)
| — 10g pumpkin seeds
| — 1 kiwi
| — cumin & salt
-----------------------

01:00 — Sleep

adjustments.reason: As logged, the day was 149 kcal under the 1,800 rest target (warn) even though protein/carbs/fat were all already in the good range. A 20g oat bump closes most of that gap (+78 kcal) and brings calories to 96% (good), without pushing carbs out of range (106.7%, still comfortably under the 125% bad line).

adjustments.from: Cumin only logged at breakfast and lunch
adjustments.reason: Protocol's anti-gas 'do' rule calls for cumin in every meal — dinner (whey, perly, nuts, pumpkin seeds, kiwi) had none. Costs nothing nutritionally.

Requested: push protein to exactly target or slightly above. Protein was sitting at 95% (157g/165g) — a half scoop extra (+15g powder, +12.5g protein) lands it at 169g (103%), just above target, without pushing any other macro out of the good range (kcal 99%, carbs 108%, fat 90%).

verdict.text_html: Protein pushed just past 100% — 1,784 kcal (99%), protein 103%, carbs 108%, fat 90% — a half extra scoop at dinner tops protein off perfectly without disturbing anything else.

coverage.name: Calories
coverage.note: The extra half scoop of whey brings this almost exactly to target too

coverage.name: Protein
coverage.note: Chicken breast 200g + 1.5 whey scoops + eggs — pushed just past 100% as requested

coverage.name: Carbs
coverage.note: Bread, oat, rice, and a whole apple land comfortably in range

coverage.name: Fat
coverage.note: Three modest 5g olive oil portions plus egg yolks — well controlled

coverage.name: Fiber
coverage.note: Oat, legume sauté, apple, and kiwi contribute, but no potato/banana today

coverage.name: Gas / bloat risk
coverage.note: Bread once, cumin now covered at all three meals, onion only in the cooked legume sauté

coverage.name: Protocol adherence
coverage.note: Bread once ✓, dinner at 21:00 ✓, cumin in every meal ✓ — cleanest adherence of the days logged so far

coverage.name: Vitamin A
coverage.note: Carrot (120g) + beldi egg yolks push this well past target

coverage.name: Iron
coverage.note: Chicken breast carries much less iron than kefta — the weakest iron day logged so far

coverage.name: Selenium
coverage.note: Chicken breast + eggs + garlic comfortably cover this

good: Protein now sits at 103% — pushed just past target with a half extra whey scoop, while carbs (108%) and fat (90%) stay comfortably in range
Protocol adherence is the best of any day so far — bread once, cumin in all three meals, dinner well before 22:00
Vitamin A (140%), Selenium (205%), Vitamin C (129%), Niacin (146%), and Biotin (250%) are all comfortably covered
Chicken breast + whole apple + garlic make for a lighter, cleaner lunch than kefta-based days
bad: Iron drops to ~44.5% — the weakest iron day logged so far, since chicken carries far less than kefta or beef
Copper (~31%) and Chromium (~13%) are both low — no kefta today to help either
Calcium (~20%), Iodine, Vitamin D (10%), Vitamin E (23%), Omega-3 (31%) — the usual structural gaps on a day with no dairy, fatty fish, or avocado
B12 at ~83% — solid for a chicken-based day, but noticeably lower than kefta days (200%+)
loggedItems: 6 egg whites + 3 egg yolks (beldi), fried with 5g olive oil
Chicken breast 200g raw, cooked with 1 tsp mustard (~5g) and 5g olive oil
300g légumes sautés (carrot 120g, courgette 120g, onion 60g) with 5g olive oil
10g mixed nuts — almonds, walnuts, cashews, pistachios (2.5g each)

fullTable.note: Chicken breast 200g + 2.5 whey scoops + eggs — pushed just past target
3 × 1L blocks from the schedule
Oat, legume sauté, apple, and kiwi contribute, but no potato/banana today
Beldi eggs' richer yolks + walnuts 2.5g — better than plain eggs but still low, no fatty fish
3 egg yolks only — structural gap without fatty fish
Chicken breast + oat + eggs — the weakest iron day logged so far, no kefta/beef today
Chicken + eggs cover most of this, but nowhere near what a kefta day provides
Oat 55g + pumpkin seeds 10g are the main sources
Only one perly nature today — no milk (protocol dont), weakest spot of the day
Chicken + oat + pumpkin seeds — solid but well under a kefta day's zinc level
Chicken + vegetables + apple + kiwi + perly, but no potato/banana today
Oat, rice, vegetables, kiwi, and yolks each contribute a little
Apple + kiwi + vegetables + garlic push this comfortably past target
Carrot (120g) + beldi egg yolks — the strongest Vitamin A day logged so far
Structural gap — no dairy, seafood, or iodized salt today
3 olive oil portions + almonds, but no avocado today
Carrot + courgette + kiwi — no avocado today to help
Chicken + garlic + rice push this just past target
Chicken breast + eggs + garlic comfortably cover this
Oat + bread + rice all contribute a little
Eggs carry most of this — chicken adds less than kefta would have
Chicken breast is a strong niacin source — comfortably past target
Nuts + chicken — modest contributions only, no kefta today
3 egg yolks carry most of this, chicken adds a bit more
Fromage frais mild — consider swapping for raib for better gut value
Bread + chicken + mustard — comfortably within normal range
Carrot, courgette, kiwi, apple, garlic, nuts, olive oil — a broad spread today
Chicken + rice + eggs + perly — just under target without kefta's usual contribution
Oat 55g + garlic + rice push this past target
Oat and rice are the only grain sources — low, as on other non-potato/banana days
Egg yolks + chicken cover this comfortably
3 egg yolks provide the bulk of this
